ddls:
CREATE TABLE "videos" (
  "video_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each video */,
  "video_duration" REAL /* Duration of the original video in seconds */,
  "original_width" INTEGER /* Width of the original video in pixels */,
  "original_height" INTEGER /* Height of the original video in pixels */,
  "original_bitrate" INTEGER /* Bit rate of the original video in bits per second */,
  "original_framerate" INTEGER /* Frame rate of the original video in frames per second */,
  "original_i_frames" INTEGER /* Number of I-frames in the original video */,
  "original_p_frames" INTEGER /* Number of P-frames in the original video */,
  "original_b_frames" INTEGER /* Number of B-frames in the original video */,
  "original_frames" INTEGER /* Total number of frames in the original video */,
  "original_size" INTEGER /* Size of the original video file in bytes */,
  "video_title" TEXT /* Title of the video */,
  "video_description" TEXT /* Description of the video */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date when the video was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the original video file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("video_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "coding_standards" (
  "coding_standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each coding standard */,
  "coding_standard_name" TEXT /* Name of the coding standard */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the coding standard */,
  "is_deprecated" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the coding standard is deprecated */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the coding standard was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("coding_standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "encoding_profiles" (
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each encoding profile */,
  "profile_name" TEXT /* Name of the encoding profile */,
  "coding_standard_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the coding standard used */,
  "target_width" INTEGER /* Target width for the encoded video in pixels */,
  "target_height" INTEGER /* Target height for the encoded video in pixels */,
  "target_bitrate" INTEGER /* Target bit rate for the encoded video in bits per second */,
  "target_framerate" INTEGER /* Target frame rate for the encoded video in frames per second */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the encoding profile */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the profile is the default profile */,
  "created_by_user_id" INTEGER /* User who created the profile */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the profile was created */,
  "last_modified_by_user_id" INTEGER /* User who last modified the profile */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the profile was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("profile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_encoding_profiles_coding_standard_id FOREIGN KEY ("coding_standard_id") REFERENCES coding_standards ("coding_standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "encoding_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each encoding session */,
  "encoded_video_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the encoded video */,
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the encoding profile used */,
  "encoding_start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the encoding session started */,
  "encoding_end_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the encoding session ended */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the encoding session */,
  "log_file_path" TEXT /* File path to the log file for the session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User who initiated the encoding session */,
  "encoding_duration" REAL /* Duration of the encoding session in seconds */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the system resources used during the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_encoding_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_encoding_sessions_profile_id FOREIGN KEY ("profile_id") REFERENCES encoding_profiles ("profile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_encoding_sessions_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES system_resources ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality metric */,
  "encoded_video_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the encoded video */,
  "psnr" REAL /* Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) */,
  "ssim" REAL /* Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) */,
  "vmaf" REAL /* Video Multimethod Assessment Fusion (VMAF) */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the quality metric */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the quality metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "video_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the video */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the video was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "access_duration" REAL /* Duration of the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_video_id FOREIGN KEY ("video_id") REFERENCES videos ("video_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "encoding_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each encoding history entry */,
  "video_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the original video */,
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the encoding profile used */,
  "encoding_date" TEXT /* Date when the encoding was performed */,
  "encoded_video_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the encoded video */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the encoding */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User who performed the encoding */,
  "encoding_duration" REAL /* Duration of the encoding process in seconds */,
  "log_file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the log file in bytes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_encoding_history_profile_id FOREIGN KEY ("profile_id") REFERENCES encoding_profiles ("profile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_encoding_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_encoding_history_video_id FOREIGN KEY ("video_id") REFERENCES videos ("video_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system resource entry */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the encoding session */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage during encoding in percentage */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage during encoding in bytes */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Disk usage during encoding in bytes */,
  "network_usage" REAL /* Network usage during encoding in bytes */,
  "gpu_usage" REAL /* GPU usage during encoding in percentage */,
  "network_latency" REAL /* Network latency during encoding in milliseconds */,
  "storage_location" TEXT /* Location of the storage used during encoding */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "encoding_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each encoding setting */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the encoding session */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the setting was created */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the setting was last modified */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the setting is the default setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_encoding_settings_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES encoding_sessions ("session_id")
)
